vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

verify.rule: All required fields are present and non-empty.
Mass estimate is within expected range for subsystem.
All P0 internal_feature_targets are represented in part_tree and geometry_ops.
All interfaces include exact neighboring subsystem labels and connection details.
Critical joints have visible locking methods and torque/preload intent.
Boundary conditions are defined for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: Potential salt-air corrosion on exposed fasteners.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coating to all external fasteners.

risk.desc: Wing spar connection may not align perfectly with wing spar.
risk.mit: Include shims or adjustable fasteners in the connection.

risk.desc: Cooling inlet may become blocked by debris during flight.
risk.mit: Design the inlet with a debris filter and regular cleaning access.

risk.desc: Service access panel may not provide sufficient clearance for maintenance.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service access panel to ensure adequate clearance.

risk.desc: Fastener patterns may not match load direction, leading to potential failure.
risk.mit: Review and adjust fastener patterns to align with load direction.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.description: Structural load path from wing spar to equipment bay door
Cooling air flow from inlet to motor pod
Thermal path from equipment bay door to wing spar
Propeller motion path from motor pylon to pusher prop
Human service access path from equipment bay door to equipment bay

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of wing spar connection to left wing
Integration of wing spar connection to right wing
Integration of motor pylon connection to motor pylon

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under 3g wing bending, 15g launch load, 2-8Nm servo load
Main spar as primary load path
Interfaces with wing skins and center joint

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present.

cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value.

verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets must appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.rule: Interfaces must have reciprocal naming and evidence.

risk.desc: Potential for salt-air corrosion affecting fasteners and interfaces.
risk.mit: Implement corrosion-resistant coatings and materials for fasteners and interfaces.

risk.desc: High-wind launch capability may strain the spar design.
risk.mit: Conduct wind tunnel tests to validate the spar's structural integrity under high-wind conditions.

risk.desc: EPP foam core may not provide sufficient stiffness for long-range surveillance missions.
risk.mit: Evaluate alternative core materials or designs that offer increased stiffness.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may cause misalignment of interfaces during temperature changes.
risk.mit: Design interfaces with thermal expansion in mind, allowing for slight misalignment.

risk.desc: Potential for payload bay to interfere with the spar's load path continuity.
risk.mit: Ensure the payload bay design does not obstruct the spar's load path continuity.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.path: Lift force from wing skin to spar, then to fuselage frames
Airflow over wing surface, around spar, through wingtip duct
Heat from electronics in wingtip, through spar to wingtip radiators
Pitch and roll of wing, transferred through spar to fuselage frames
External air pressure on wing skin, through spar to wingtip pressure sensors
Wiring harness from wingtip electronics, through spar to fuselage electronics bay
Access to spar for maintenance, through wingtip access hatch

risk.desc: The design may not be salt-air resistant due to material choice.
risk.mit: Conduct salt-air resistance testing and apply appropriate coatings.

risk.desc: High-wind launch capability may be compromised by control surface design.
risk.mit: Review and optimize control surface design for high-wind conditions.

risk.desc: Lightening pockets may weaken the rib structure if not properly designed.
risk.mit: Ensure lightening pockets are optimized for structural integrity.

risk.desc: Leading edge sock may not provide sufficient aerodynamic performance.
risk.mit: Optimize leading edge sock design for improved aerodynamic performance.

risk.desc: Trailing edge taper may affect lift distribution.
risk.mit: Review and optimize trailing edge taper for optimal lift distribution.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges

physics_paths.description: Wing bending loads from lift and gusts are transferred to fuselage via spar sockets and wing root attachment points.
Airflow over NACA airfoil generates lift, with control surfaces providing directional control.
Heat from solar radiation and air friction is dissipated through the wing rib's surface.
Control surfaces rotate about hinge lines for pitch and roll control.
Access to control surface hinges and wing rib internals for maintenance and inspection.

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of control surface hinges and winglet structures.
Integration of spar sockets and skin sockets for wing root attachment and skin fitting.
Optimization of airfoil section and leading edge sock for improved aerodynamic performance.

verification_focus.description: Verify that lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer.
Verify that adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm.
Verify that cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
Verify that critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
Verify that all dimensions are physically plausible for the subsystem scale.

generated_parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
generated_parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

requirements: Design and manufacture an elevon servo pocket for a fixed-wing UAV.
The servo pocket must house the MG90S or DS215MG servo, control horn, and pushrod channel.
Provide a removable access cover for maintenance and inspection.
Ensure the servo pocket is salt-air resistant and capable of withstanding high-wind launches.
The design must be compatible with the wing structure and control surface.

risk.desc: Potential for salt-air corrosion on exposed fasteners.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coatings or use stainless steel fasteners.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain during high-g launch.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief features are present at all cable exits.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may affect fit between components.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion coefficients in fit_class calculations.

risk.desc: Potential for control surface binding due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter fit_class tolerances for control surface interfaces.

risk.desc: Risk of access cover detachment during flight.
risk.mit: Increase fastener preload or use locking mechanisms.

physics_paths.description: Servo force to control horn
Heat dissipation from servo to atmosphere
Electrical harness routing

integration_opportunities.description: Control horn mount for servo output

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile
